methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Type One Energy, a Series B nuclear energy company founded by John Canik, has raised $100M+ and carries a last post-money valuation of $200M+. The nuclear energy sector includes 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-later-stage advancement, anchoring to the $200M post-money base and implying a forward valuation of $380M. Peer-median comparison is not applicable: while 7 same-sector peers exist, the nuclear energy space lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables (fewer than 3 datapoints met filtering thresholds), making narrow-set median analysis unreliable. Broadening to all-sector medians would dilute sector-specific signal and introduce material bias.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $323M (low) to $437M (high), with a median of $380M. This represents approximately 1.6x to 2.2x the current $200M valuation. Key uncertainties include technology maturity and regulatory pathway clarity—nuclear projects face extended commercialization timelines and capital requirements not fully captured in early-stage comparable multiples. The narrow peer dataset also limits confidence; sector maturation and additional nuclear-focused fundraising will strengthen future peer-based benchmarking.
